channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Higashioka's homer starts rally as Padres beat Braves 5-4 to sweep NL Wild Card Series
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Kyle Higashioka's solo homer started a five-run rally against an ailing Max Fried with two outs in the second inning, and the San Diego Padres held on to sweep the Atlanta Braves with a 5-4 victory in Game 2 of their NL Wild Card Series on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: BERNIE WILSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Orioles get swept out of the playoffs in their only series for a second consecutive year
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Things went so poorly for the Baltimore Orioles in the playoffs that even when they had the bases loaded and a hit by pitch scores a run, Colton Cowser swung at a ball thrown right at him, missed and broke his left hand in the process.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: What position is Jonathan Kuminga? Entering Year 4, answers still diverge
item.description: Jonathan Kuminga thinks he's a small forward. So does Draymond Green. But how Steve Kerr deploys him remains a question.
item.dc:creator: Danny Emerman, The Mercury News

item.title: Zach LaVine still a Chicago Bull (for now), says he's ready to do whatever teams asks of him this season
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Zach LaVine insisted he is healthy and ready to do whatever the Chicago Bulls ask of him this season. Above all, he's ready to put the drama in the past.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Dodgers issue statement on Fernando Valenzuela's status
item.description: Dodgers broadcaster and former player Fernando Valenzuela has been hospitalized and will miss the playoffs this year, but the team is hopeful he will return for next season.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, KTLA 5

item.title: Michael Jordan's team and another sue NASCAR over revenue sharing model
item.description: Two NASCAR teams — one of them owned by Michael Jordan — filed a federal antitrust lawsuit against the stock car series and chairman Jim France on Wednesday, claiming the new charter system limits competition by unfairly binding teams to the series, its tracks and its suppliers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: From food to murder, Aaron Rodgers talks all things Berkeley ahead of College GameDay appearance
item.description: (KRON) -- The college football world will have its eyes on Berkeley this weekend as Cal hosts ESPN's famous College GameDay show for the first time before its game against No. 8 Miami on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: Legendary Dodgers pitcher, broadcaster Fernando Valenzuela hospitalized
item.description: Dodgers broadcaster Fernando Valenzuela has been hospitalized ahead of the team's playoff run. The popular Spanish-language broadcaster and former Dodger star pitcher stepped away from his playcalling duties last week, though no reason was given at the time, according to the Orange County Register.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, KTLA 5

item.title: Are professional athletes playing too many games?
item.description: Fans, athletes and medical experts have been having discussions on the increasing demand for players starting from the time they first start playing, all the way up to the professional ranks.
item.dc:creator: Juan Cisneros, Fox 5

item.title: Utah Jazz veterans hope to lead young core
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - There are no less than ten players 23 years of age or younger currently on the Utah Jazz roster. So for veterans like Jordan Clarkson, they know their role as leaders is invaluable.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Column: Lightning and thunder could bode well for Bears' offense
item.description: The Chicago Bears have developed a formidable 1-2 punch in running backs D'Andre Swift and Roschon Johnson, who have shown the ability to both smash and slice through defenses, but the success of the offense depends on the performance of the offensive line.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Dikembe Mutombo, a Hall of Fame player, dies at 58 from brain cancer
item.description: Mutombo, a Basketball Hall of Famer who was one of the best defensive players in NBA history, died Monday.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The final day for the Oakland Athletics arrives ahead of next season's move away from the Bay
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — When manager Mark Kotsay removed his Oakland jersey for the final time Sunday, it was bound for the Baseball Hall of Fame. Brent Rooker? His final jersey with the name Oakland across the front will be preserved in a frame and displayed on his wall at home.
item.dc:creator: TIM BOOTH, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: College GameDay makes first-ever appearance at Berkeley
item.description: (KRON) -- Since taking its show on the road back in 1993, "College GameDay" has been a huge presence in the college football community by being known to travel across dozens of campuses across the country.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: Chastain passes Truex on late restart to win NASCAR Cup Series race at Kansas Speedway
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Kan. (AP) — Ross Chastain finally got to smash a watermelon for the first time this season Sunday at Kansas Speedway. Smashed the hopes of a bunch of playoff drivers trying to earn an automatic berth in the next round, too.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Broncos running back Tyler Badie carted from sideline with a back injury
item.description: Denver Broncos running back Tyler Badie was placed on a back board and carted from the sideline before the start of the second quarter against the New York Jets on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 2025 Club World Cup final to be at MetLife Stadium, site of 2026 World Cup final
item.description: Next year’s championship of the expanded Club World Cup will be played on July 13 at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey, site of the 2026 World Cup final.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: White Sox look to break Tigers' home win streak
item.description: Chicago White Sox (39-121, fifth in the AL Central) vs. Detroit Tigers (86-74, second in the AL Central) Detroit; Saturday, 1:10 p.m. EDT PITCHING PROBABLES: White Sox: Sean Burke (0-0); Tigers: TBD BOTTOM LINE: The Detroit Tigers, on a five-game home winning streak, host the Chicago White Sox.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: SJSU opponent forfeits volleyball match, lawsuit alleges player is transgender
item.description: (KRON) -- Boise State University's volleyball team forfeited a match against San Jose State, the school announced on Friday. The cancellation came after one San Jose State player said in a lawsuit that one of her teammates is transgender.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: A's beat Rangers 3-2 in Coliseum's last game
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (KRON) -- In their last game at the Oakland Coliseum, the A's beat the Texas Rangers 3-2 on Thursday afternoon. The A's took a 2-0 lead in the third inning and did not relinquish it.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4
